join_order.build: sub(sic[], cik[]) — full arrays loaded; adsh_qualifying_bitset and adsh_to_cik_idx built
join_order.notes: sub_sic (172KB) and sub_cik (344KB) fully loaded. adsh_qualifying_bitset = bitset<86135> (~10KB, L1-resident): set bit i if sub_sic[i] BETWEEN 4000-4999. adsh_to_cik_idx[i] = cik_remap_index (uint16_t) for qualifying adsh_codes, UINT16_MAX otherwise.
tag_abstract (1MB) fully loaded; O(1) by tagver_code; abstract==0 ~95% pass

join_order.build: eq_count_map (FlatCountMap, 65536 slots, 786KB) — SIC-pre-filtered EQ pairs only
join_order.notes: Pre scan filters by adsh_qualifying_bitset AND stmt==eq_code: ~42K keys (vs 1.06M before). FlatCountMap cap_hint=25000 -> 65536 slots x 12B = 786KB (L2/L3-resident). No bloom filter needed — direct probe is cache-resident. Single-threaded insert: ~42K x 3ns = <1ms (vs 1.06M x 30ns = 32ms before).

filters.notes: Zone maps skip entire 100K-row blocks; row-level check applied first
Most selective — applied after uom+tagver checks via O(1) sub_sic[adsh_code] array lookup
eq_count_map now only contains SIC-qualified EQ pairs (~42K entries). Direct map probe (786KB, L2/L3-resident). Bloom filter eliminated.

predicate_order_per_num_row: 1. uom_code == usd_code (zone map at block level, then row check)
3. sub_sic[adsh_code] BETWEEN 4000 AND 4999 (most selective, array O(1))
5. eq_count_map.get(key) != 0 (786KB, L2/L3-resident, no bloom pre-check needed)

data_structures.sub_sic_array: flat_array(86135 x int16_t) — direct indexed by adsh_code
data_structures.sub_cik_array: flat_array(86135 x int32_t) — direct indexed by adsh_code
data_structures.adsh_qualifying_bitset: bitset of 86135 bits (~10KB, L1-resident): bit i set iff sub_sic[i] BETWEEN 4000-4999; used during pre scan to pre-filter eq_count_map entries
data_structures.adsh_to_cik_idx: flat_array(86135 x uint16_t = 172KB): for qualifying adsh_codes maps to CIK remap index [0, N_ciks); UINT16_MAX for non-qualifying; built during sub scan
data_structures.cik_remap: vector<int32_t> idx_to_cik (N_ciks entries, N_ciks <= 3445): maps remap index -> original CIK value; built during sub scan by collecting distinct CIK values with sic 4000-4999
data_structures.tag_abstract_array: flat_array(1070662 x int8_t) — direct indexed by tagver_code
data_structures.eq_count_map: FlatCountMap with cap_hint=25000 -> 65536 slots x 12B = 786KB (L2/L3-resident); key=((uint64_t)(uint32_t)adsh_code<<32)|(uint32_t)tagver_code; value=uint32_t count of EQ pre rows; SIC-pre-filtered so only ~42K entries
data_structures.agg_map_per_thread: unordered_map<uint64_t, AggState> per thread; AggState = {double sum, int64_t cnt, vector<uint64_t> cik_bits(cik_words)}; cik_bits is a compact bitset of N_ciks bits (ceil(N_ciks/64) uint64_t words); reserve(1024) per thread
data_structures.aggregation: thread-local hash maps merged via parallel binary-tree reduction (6 rounds for 64 threads); CIK merge is bitwise OR over cik_words uint64_ts — no allocation, no sorting, no std::merge

load_sub_arrays_and_build_cik_remap: Build adsh_qualifying_bitset[86135] (~10KB): for i in [0,SUB_N): if sub_sic[i] BETWEEN 4000-4999, set bit i
Build cik_to_idx unordered_map and idx_to_cik vector: for each qualifying adsh_code i, look up sub_cik[i]; if not seen, assign next index. N_ciks = idx_to_cik.size() <= 3445
Build adsh_to_cik_idx[86135]: uint16_t array, initialized to UINT16_MAX; for qualifying adsh i: adsh_to_cik_idx[i] = cik_to_idx[sub_cik[i]]

build_eq_count_map_with_sic_prefilter.strategy: parallel scan with adsh_qualifying_bitset pre-filter AND stmt==eq_code; thread-local key vectors; single-threaded insert into FlatCountMap
build_eq_count_map_with_sic_prefilter.per_thread_filter: for each pre row i: if (pre_stmt_code[i] == eq_code) AND (adsh_qualifying_bitset[pre_adsh_code[i]] is set): emit key = ((uint64_t)(uint32_t)pre_adsh_code[i]<<32)|(uint32_t)pre_tagver_code[i]; skip if key==UINT64_MAX
build_eq_count_map_with_sic_prefilter.merge_into_map: single-threaded loop over thread vectors calling eq_count_map.increment(k); ~42K insertions into 786KB L2/L3-resident map (<1ms vs 32ms before)
build_eq_count_map_with_sic_prefilter.no_bloom_filter: bloom filter eliminated — eq_count_map at 786KB is L2/L3-resident; direct probe latency <3ns vs 30ns for old 25MB map

accumulators: cik_bits: vector<uint64_t>(cik_words) — compact bitset of N_ciks bits; set bit adsh_to_cik_idx[adsh_code] for each qualifying num row
aggregation.cik_insert: g.cik_bits[cik_idx >> 6] |= 1ULL << (cik_idx & 63)  // O(1), no allocation, no sort
aggregation.merge_strategy: parallel binary-tree reduction (6 rounds for 64 threads). For each merged entry: sum+=src.sum; cnt+=src.cnt; for j in [0,cik_words): dst.cik_bits[j] |= src.cik_bits[j]. Bitwise OR replaces std::merge+std::unique+allocation.
aggregation.having: retain groups where popcount(cik_bits) >= 2  (popcount over cik_words uint64_ts)
aggregation.cik_count_for_output: __builtin_popcountll over cik_bits vector gives exact COUNT(DISTINCT cik)
aggregation.post_having_decode: iterate global_agg[0], decode tlabel via tlabel_offsets+tlabel_data[tagver_code], re-aggregate by (sic,tlabel) string key (CIK merge via bitwise OR on cik_bits), then popcount for final count, HAVING+sort+limit

parallelism.merge_strategy: parallel binary-tree reduction (6 rounds for 64 threads); CIK merge is bitwise OR — O(cik_words) = O(54) per group, no heap allocation

indexes.usage: skip block b if zm[b].min_uom > usd_code || zm[b].max_uom < usd_code; struct ZoneMap { int8_t min_uom, max_uom; int32_t min_ddate, max_ddate; } sizeof=12 (2-byte pad)

output.tlabel_decode: after primary aggregation merge, decode tlabel for each group from tlabel_offsets+tlabel_data by tagver_code; re-aggregate by (sic, tlabel) string to merge groups sharing the same tlabel; CIK merge via bitwise OR on cik_bits; then popcount for exact cik count, HAVING cik_count>=2, sort by total_value DESC, limit 500

optimization_notes: Two root causes diagnosed. (1) Category A — logical plan restructuring: the previous plan built eq_count_map from ALL pre rows with stmt='EQ' (~1.06M entries, 25MB, 2M slots), but 96% of those have non-qualifying SIC codes. Fix: build a 10KB adsh_qualifying_bitset from sub_sic during preprocessing; during the parallel pre scan, check bitset[pre_adsh_code] AND stmt==eq_code before emitting a key. This reduces eq_count_map entries from 1.06M to ~42K, shrinking the map from 25MB (LLC-resident) to 786KB (L2/L3-resident). Single-threaded insert drops from ~32ms (1.06M x 30ns LLC access) to <1ms (42K x 3ns L2 access). The bloom filter is eliminated entirely — a 786KB map is already effectively cache-resident. (2) Category B — algorithm change for CIK tracking: the previous sorted vector<int32_t> approach required O(log k) lower_bound + O(k) insert per row, and std::merge + std::unique + heap allocation during tree-reduction merges. Since only CIK values from adsh_codes with sic 4000-4999 can qualify (at most 3445 distinct CIKs), we remap qualifying CIKs to indices [0, N_ciks) and track them with a compact uint64_t bitset (54 words = 432 bytes per group). Insertion becomes a single bit-set (O(1), no allocation); merge becomes 54 bitwise OR operations (vs std::merge+unique+allocation); final count uses __builtin_popcountll. The adsh_to_cik_idx array (86135 x uint16_t = 172KB, LLC-resident) provides O(1) lookup from adsh_code to CIK remap index. Expected speedup: build_joins 70ms -> ~15ms, merge_aggregates 36ms -> ~8ms, main_scan 49ms -> ~38ms (eq_count_map probe now L2-resident, no bloom computation), total target ~80-90ms.
